description:
Goal
Replace return facts that pretend a ready `Ty` always exists with explicit `ReturnEvidence`, then make semantic-analysis value evidence explicit enough that call resolution and artifacts cannot accidentally project unavailable information. A missing fact means no current evidence has been produced. A produced fact says what was actually learned: known concrete return type, explicit dynamic boundary evidence, or no-return control flow. No consumer should infer readiness from `Option<Ty>`, `none`, or `any`.

Signal
- The work is executed through the child tickets in dependency order; this parent is a plan container, not a one-commit task.
- `FactKey::ReturnType` and `return_type.defined` are removed or deliberately renamed to `ReturnEvidence` / `return_evidence.defined` with a payload that is not just a bare `Ty`.
- `ActivationMap` stores explicit return evidence; activation-cycle ascent applies only to the known-type component.
- Local semantic values are explicit evidence variants, not `SemanticValue { flow_ty: Option<Ty>, observed_ty: Option<Ty> }` or equivalent readiness-by-`Option`.
- Semantic callers can publish demand/activation evidence without a return type when the return is not necessary, but wait or withhold typed outputs when absent return evidence blocks the job.
- Downstream completeness consumers wait on settled return evidence and project to `Ty` only at named artifact/ABI/dynamic boundaries.
- Explicit dynamic/top and no-return/bottom remain possible only as named semantic evidence, not as readiness placeholders.

Strategy
1. `.6.1`: introduce the return-evidence payload end-to-end at the activation fact boundary.
2. `.6.5`: replace local semantic pending `Option<Ty>` with explicit value evidence before tightening call behavior.
3. `.6.2`: make direct/protocol/closure call resolution stop or publish less when required return evidence is absent.
4. `.6.3`: move `Ty` projection to explicit artifact/backend/native boundaries that truly need a type-shaped value.
5. `.6.4`: delete old `ReturnType` naming, telemetry, tests, and docs residue before `any`/`none` cleanup begins.

```mermaid
graph LR
  A[fz-rh2.18.6.1 ReturnEvidence payload] --> B[fz-rh2.18.6.5 local semantic evidence]
  B --> C[fz-rh2.18.6.2 call resolution waits]
  C --> D[fz-rh2.18.6.3 artifact projections]
  D --> E[fz-rh2.18.6.4 residue deletion]
```

Outro
No `ReturnType`-as-ready-`Ty`, no `Option<Ty>` return slot standing for readiness, no local `Option<Ty>` readiness, no absent-callee-as-`none`, no unknown-callee-as-`any`, no recursive-function switch for return widening, no cyclic widening outside the evidence domain, no compatibility API that makes ReturnEvidence look like old ReturnType, and no downstream consumer that infers readiness from a type value.

comments.text: Reordered behind fz-rh2.18.9 because ReturnEvidence should land on top of activation keys that already separate identity partition from input evidence. Otherwise it risks preserving raw-evidence keying under a cleaner return-fact name.

title: Compiler2 semantics: replace ReturnType with ReturnEvidence
